result.markdown:
# Customize ER plots This page demonstrates the options available in `plot_er_gof()` and how to customize ER plots beyond the built-in options. See also the [function reference](https://genentech.github.io/BayesERtools/reference/plot_er_gof.html) for complete documentation. ## Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) library(BayesERtools) library(here) theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 12)) ``` ::: ## Data and model ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} data(d_sim_binom_cov) d_sim_binom_cov_2 <- d_sim_binom_cov |> mutate( AUCss_1000 = AUCss / 1000, Dose = glue::glue("{Dose_mg} mg") ) df_er_ae_hgly2 <- d_sim_binom_cov_2 |> filter(AETYPE == "hgly2") ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} set.seed(1234) ermod_bin <- dev_ermod_bin( data = df_er_ae_hgly2, var_resp = "AEFLAG", var_exposure = "AUCss_1000" ) ``` ::: ## `plot_er_gof()` options ### Default plot ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-default-1.png){#fig-default width=672} ::: ::: ### Grouping by dose (`var_group`) Color data points and add boxplot by dose group: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-var-group-1.png){#fig-var-group width=672} ::: ::: ### Show exposure coefficient CI (`show_coef_exp`) Display the credible interval for the exposure coefficient: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_coef_exp = TRUE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-coef-exp-1.png){#fig-coef-exp width=672} ::: ::: Customize the position and size of the coefficient label: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof( ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_coef_exp = TRUE, coef_pos_x = 2, coef_pos_y = 0.2, coef_size = 3 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-coef-exp-pos-1.png){#fig-coef-exp-pos width=672} ::: ::: ### Number of bins for observed probability (`n_bins`) Change the number of bins used to summarize observed event rates: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", n_bins = 6) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-n-bins-1.png){#fig-n-bins width=672} ::: ::: ### Confidence interval widths Control CI widths for observed data summary (`qi_width_obs`), simulated curve (`qi_width_sim`), and coefficient (`qi_width_coef`): ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof( ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_coef_exp = TRUE, qi_width_obs = 0.9, qi_width_sim = 0.9, qi_width_coef = 0.9 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-qi-width-1.png){#fig-qi-width width=672} ::: ::: ### Boxplot options Control boxplot visibility and appearance: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Explicitly add boxplot without var_group plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, add_boxplot = TRUE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-boxplot-options-1.png){#fig-boxplot-options width=672} ::: ::: Adjust boxplot height and show y-axis title: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof( ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", boxplot_height = 0.25, show_boxplot_y_title = TRUE ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-boxplot-height-1.png){#fig-boxplot-height width=672} ::: ::: ### Caption (`show_caption`) The caption is shown by default. Hide it with `show_caption = FALSE`: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_caption = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-no-caption-1.png){#fig-no-caption width=672} ::: ::: ### Using log scale for exposure Use `*` instead of `+` to apply scales to all panels in the combined plot: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_coef_exp = TRUE) * xgxr::xgx_scale_x_log10(guide = guide_axis(minor.ticks = TRUE)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-log-scale-1.png){#fig-log-scale width=672} ::: ::: ## Customize each element of GOF plot For deeper customization (titles, themes, colors, shapes), use `return_components = TRUE` to get modifiable ggplot objects, then recombine with `combine_er_components()`. ### Basic workflow ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Get components comps <- plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", return_components = TRUE) # Modify the main plot comps$main <- comps$main + labs(title = "Exposure-Response Analysis", x = "AUC (ng·h/mL / 1000)") # Recombine combine_er_components(comps) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-return-components-1.png){#fig-return-components width=672} ::: ::: ### Customizing themes, colors, and shapes ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} comps <- plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", return_components = TRUE) # Extract original data for custom point layer origdata <- extract_data(ermod_bin) |> mutate(Dose = glue::glue("{Dose_mg} mg")) comps$main <- comps$main + # Add points with custom shapes by dose geom_jitter( data = origdata, aes(x = AUCss_1000, y = AEFLAG, color = Dose, shape = Dose), width = 0, height = 0.05, alpha = 0.7 ) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15)) + scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) + labs(title = "Custom Theme with Shapes", color = "Dose", shape = "Dose") + theme_minimal() ``` 
Scale for colour is already present.
Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
```{.r .cell-code} comps$boxplot <- comps$boxplot + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") + scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") + theme_minimal() combine_er_components(comps) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-custom-theme-1.png){#fig-custom-theme width=672} ::: ::: ### Adjusting heights and caption ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} comps <- plot_er_gof( ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", show_caption = TRUE, return_components = TRUE ) comps$main <- comps$main + labs(title = "Adjusted Layout") # Custom heights and suppress caption combine_er_components(comps, heights = c(0.7, 0.3), add_caption = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-combine-options-1.png){#fig-combine-options width=672} ::: ::: ### Applying log scale to components ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} comps <- plot_er_gof(ermod_bin, var_group = "Dose", return_components = TRUE) log_scale <- xgxr::xgx_scale_x_log10(guide = guide_axis(minor.ticks = TRUE)) comps$main <- comps$main + log_scale + labs(title = "Log Scale") comps$boxplot <- comps$boxplot + log_scale combine_er_components(comps) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](plot_customize_files/figure-html/fig-log-scale-components-1.png){#fig-log-scale-components width=672} ::: ::: ## Summary For complete documentation, see the [`plot_er_gof()` reference](https://genentech.github.io/BayesERtools/reference/plot_er_gof.html). **`plot_er_gof()` options:** | Option | Description | Default | |--------|-------------|---------| | `var_group` | Group data by column | `NULL` | | `add_boxplot` | Add exposure boxplot | `TRUE` if `var_group` set | | `boxplot_height` | Relative height of boxplot | `0.15` | | `show_boxplot_y_title` | Show boxplot y-axis title | `FALSE` | | `n_bins` | Bins for observed probability | `4` | | `qi_width_obs` | CI width for observed data | `0.95` | | `qi_width_sim` | CI width for model curve | `0.95` | | `show_coef_exp` | Show coefficient CI | `FALSE` | | `coef_pos_x/y` | Coefficient label position | Auto | | `coef_size` | Coefficient label size | `4` | | `qi_width_coef` | CI width for coefficient | `0.95` | | `show_caption` | Show plot caption | `TRUE` | | `return_components` | Return components for customization | `FALSE` | **Tips:** - Use `*` instead of `+` to apply scales to all panels - Use `return_components = TRUE` for titles, themes, colors, and shapes - Components: `$main` (ER plot), `$boxplot`, `$caption` - Use `combine_er_components()` to recombine after modification
